model.label: DeepSeek V3.2
model.description: DeepSeek • General-purpose V-series model optimized for reasoning, coding, and efficient inference.

pick.rationale: Florida's status as the defending national champion and No. 1 seed is backed by elite efficiency, ranking 4th nationally with a top-5 defense and top-10 offense. The Gators' championship experience and the high-level competition of the SEC provide a significant edge over an Iowa team with a weaker defensive profile. While Iowa's solid offense can keep them competitive, Florida's balanced attack and defensive intensity should control the game and secure a spot in the Sweet 16.

reasoningStep.title: Round of 32: Florida vs Iowa Evaluation
reasoningStep.summary: Florida's superior team quality, elite defense, and championship pedigree outweigh Iowa's offensive capabilities.
evidence: Efficiency ratings show Florida ranked 4th (Torvik/KenPom) with adjDE ranked 5th and adjOE ranked 11th/9th, while Iowa is ranked 25th-26th with adjDE ranked 35th and adjOE ranked 29th/31st. No major injury concerns were found for either team. Prior picks established Florida's dominant first-round win as the 1-seed, while Iowa advanced as a lower seed. The significant gap in defensive efficiency and overall team quality favors Florida.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

modelTrace.content:
I can fetch the Florida Wikipedia page for roster and season info. But maybe I should look at matchup specifics: Florida's elite defense vs Iowa's good offense; Florida's elite offense vs Iowa's decent defense. The gap in defensive efficiency is large: Florida's adjde rank 5 vs Iowa's 35. That suggests Florida can limit Iowa's scoring. Florida's offense is also better (rank 11 vs 29). The overall efficiency margin difference is substantial: Florida's adj EM ~33.8, Iowa's ~22.4, difference ~11.4 points per 100 possessions.

Iowa's path to victory: They need to shoot well from three, force turnovers, and perhaps get Florida into foul trouble. Florida is the defending national champion and #1 seed in the South. They have high-major experience and are battle-tested in SEC. Iowa is a 9-seed (assuming they beat Clemson, which they did per prior pick). They are a solid Big Ten team but not elite.

I should also consider that Florida already played a First Four winner (Lehigh) and won comfortably. Iowa had a tougher 8/9 game against Clemson. Could fatigue be a factor? Iowa's game might have been more physically demanding. However, both teams have similar rest.

Let me search for any analysis of this specific matchup.
